title: Quick check: Parallel and intercepted routes

questions.prompt: What's the killer feature of parallel routes (slots) for dashboards?

choices.text: Independent loading and error states per slot. A `@analytics` slot has its own loading.tsx and error.tsx; a `@team` slot has its own. The analytics slot's spinner shows independently of team's; an error in analytics doesn't break team's render. Each slot's data fetches in parallel; slow slots don't block fast ones. Combined with bookmarkable URL state per slot, this is dramatically cleaner than building one giant dashboard page. Receive both `children` and named slot props in the layout.

questions.explanation: Independent loading, error, and data fetching per slot — perfect for dashboards.

questions.prompt: Photo gallery: clicking a photo should show a modal, but the URL should change and the modal URL should be bookmarkable. Pattern?

choices.text: Intercepted routes. Create a normal `app/photos/[id]/page.tsx` for the full-page view, plus `app/@modal/(..)photos/[id]/page.tsx` that intercepts the same URL from `/photos` context and renders a modal. Click from grid → modal opens, URL updates to /photos/123, grid stays rendered underneath. Direct link to /photos/123 → full-page view renders. Bookmarkable both ways. `router.back()` from the modal closes it. The `(..)` syntax means 'intercept from one level up.'

questions.explanation: Intercepted routes (`(..)`) render one route in another's context. Same URL = two renders.
